1.question: According to James, what does the trying of your faith work?

1.title: The First Vision verse
1.body: James 1:5 — "If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God" — is the verse that inspired the young Joseph Smith to pray in 1820, leading to the First Vision.

1.question: According to James, what is pure and undefiled religion?
options: To visit the fatherless and widows and keep oneself unspotted from the world
To speak much about the law

2.title: A gold ring and vile raiment
2.body: James paints a concrete scene of worship: the rich man with a gold ring is offered a good place; the poor man, to stand or sit on the floor. Such favoritism makes believers 'judges of evil thoughts.'

2.question: By making a distinction between rich and poor, what do believers become, according to verse 4?

2.template: the royal law according to the scripture, Thou shalt love thy {0} as thyself, ye do well

2.question: According to verse 13, what rejoiceth against judgment?

2.template: Even so faith, if it hath not {0}, is dead, being alone.

2.title: Even the devils believe
2.body: Believing 'there is one God' is well, but James notes 'the devils also believe, and tremble.' Mere belief, without works, does not set the faithful apart from a devil.

pairs.right: Offered his son Isaac upon the altar
Received the messengers and sent them out another way
Was called the Friend of God

2.template: as the body without the {0} is dead, so faith without works is dead also.

3.title: Why few masters?
3.body: James warns that those who teach receive greater responsibility and judgment, for their words sway many — so his concern over the tongue begins right in this exhortation.

4.title: The war within
4.body: James locates the source of wars and fightings not in outward circumstances but in the lusts that 'war' within a person.

4.question: According to verse 2, why do they have not?

4.title: Plans without God
4.body: James rebukes those who plan journeys and profits with full confidence, forgetting that tomorrow is not in their hands.

5.body: Verse 14 describes calling for the elders of the church to pray over the sick, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord — an ordinance of healing still observed by the laying on of hands.
